canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, ImageNetV2 is consistently characterized as a test set or benchmark derived from ImageNet. The papers offer slightly different but related framings of its purpose. One definition states it is used to test "robustness under natural distribution shift." Another paper describes it as a tool for measuring "generalization to new, similarly distributed data," specifying that it was collected with a replicated protocol. In terms of its application, it is used for model evaluations, sometimes in conjunction with other datasets. As one study notes, "We use the ImageNetV2, ImageNet-R, ImageNet-A, and ImageNetSketch datasets for these evaluations".
papers: Mitigate the Gap: Improving Cross-Modal Alignment in CLIP
Revisit Large-Scale Image-Caption Data in Pre-training Multimodal Foundation Models
DEEM: Diffusion models serve as the eyes of large language models for image perception
LiNeS: Post-training Layer Scaling Prevents Forgetting and Enhances Model Merging

definitions.text: A distribution-shift benchmark derived from ImageNet used to test robustness under natural distribution shift.
definitions.paper: Mitigate the Gap: Improving Cross-Modal Alignment in CLIP

definitions.text: A test set for ImageNet collected with a replicated protocol to measure generalization to new, similarly distributed data.
definitions.paper: LiNeS: Post-training Layer Scaling Prevents Forgetting and Enhances Model Merging
